title: Premarket AI Trading Analyst
author: Fun_Crazy_812
selftext:
Hey everyone,

  
I'm a discretionary day trader primarily trading NQ, and I was looking to build a premarket AI analyst that handles the market analysis side of things for me. I want it to go through data connectors and API's, aggregates the data, and gives me probabilities for different scenarios. The only problem is, I'm relatively new to coding and automating, and I don't know which data connectors I should use (the only one I know for certain I'm using is FRED). Primarily I'm looking for data connectors regarding these inputs:



1. Breaking News, Macro Context

  
2. Economic Data

  
3. Themes Driving the Markets

  
4. GEX Snapshots and Dealer Positioning

  
5. Market Conditions, Overall Sentiment (VXN, VIX, Risk Sentiment, Positioning)

  
Also, how many data connectors is a good amount without causing the AI model to hallucinate? Thanks, if you guys also have any tips, answers, or recommendations, please comment.

comments.author: The_AI_Trader
comments.body:
The AI can handle a lot of context. 

I’m part of a team that built SkyAnalyst AI. Look it up. It can give you an idea of how much info the AI can handle . 

Regardless of how much data it can handle, we saw that it’s very important to load technical indicator data across multiple time frames . A full CMT array of indicator date ( Ema , VWAP, vol, rsi, MACD, fibs, atr, session h/l, support-resistance, as well as macro context technical data: Vix, breadth, 5 day emas on oil, rates, Vix, and others ) on 5, 15, 60 min candles. 

This plus outputs of macro and trend agents using similar technical data to do their own análisis. 

Hope this helps.

replies.author: ja_trader
replies.body: ahh, the sky ai guy again

replies.author: Fun_Crazy_812
replies.body: lmao 90% of the comments are just shillers on reddit now

comments.author: Imaginary_Ad_974
comments.body:
Early this year, I used Claude Code to build a similar market research analyst agent for my Options Trading.

My agent gives me the single best trading strategy for a given ticker and a given objective (bullish bet, bearish bet, earn income, harvest volatility) based on a wide range of external information, live price/market data, and real time price/IV/greeks data.  

Before providing a trade recommendation, it backtests, forward tests, and runs 1,000 Monte Carlo simulations on the strategy and provides the results along with its logic, compares it to other alternative strategies, and provides details on sizing, entry, and exit instructions that respect earnings dates and FOMC calendar, et al.

One-time cost for hardware:  Mac Mini $599

Total Claude Code token cost per trade recommendation:  <$0.50.

Total token cost to build the agent:  <$100 (so far).

Cost of live data:  $0.00 - my agent built and maintains its own real time market data infrastructure, calculates its own Greeks using Black Scholes, et al.  All for free.

I have been amazed at my agent’s ability to factor-in everything I used to research before making a trade - plus a lot more risk factors I never had time to consider like CORS, Gamma exposure, yield curve movement, currency momentum, IV/IVR, contango, etc etc.

It reads daily price charts perfectly, as well.

This agent is saving me hours per week and so far has helped me generate consistent profits using 20-90DTE contracts (I don’t day trade / scalp).  

I mostly use options for hedging my long term portfolio, for mean reversion quick wins, and for earning long term income.

I’m sure you could build a similar research agent for swing trading futures for the costs above.  

If you trade intraday, however, the data costs will be higher, esp if you rely on L2.

replies.author: Fun_Crazy_812
replies.body: Thanks so much for your answer! Sounds like a really detailed system, thanks for giving me a framework of what worked for you. I have a question though, do you have a scoring system for the AI? Ive been doing a little research lately, and Im seeing that instead of the AI giving you a non deterministic answer based on the data ingested, adding a “deterministic factor” as well can really help the AI prevent hallucinations (so for example in your case, the AI scores which of your strategies work best from 1-100). But insane system, I honestly needed some ideas from someone else, thanks so much (also I do have L2 data with rithmic)

replies.author: Imaginary_Ad_974
replies.body:
My Options Agent is designed to recommend new trades, backtest/analyze them thoroughly, select the best single trade to make, and provide a full 360-degree report on why that trade is the best available in current market conditions for a given ticker, timeframe, profit goal, and maximum risk. 

For this reason, I don't ask my agent to grade its choices on a 1-100 score.  I'd rather it explain its thinking in detail.  
  
In every Trade Recommendation, the agent provides a shortlist of the trades it considered and compares them on a set of key metrics.    


Here is an example of a recent trade recommendation I generated using my agent:  

# 🐻 SMH Bear Call Spread — Full Trade Plan[](http://localhost:8501/#igv-bullish-options-trade-full-research-report)

**Date:** May 27, 2026 | **Spot:** 590.14∣∗∗Account:∗∗590.14∣∗∗*Account*:∗∗26,776 (flat — no existing SMH exposure)

# 1. ✅ IV Snapshot Confirmed (Live)[](http://localhost:8501/#critical-pre-trade-flag-options-liquidity-warning)

|Metric|Value|Signal|
|:-|:-|:-|
|ATM IV|**51.7%**|Elevated|
|HV 20d|**40.4%**|IV premium = +11.3pts|
|IV Rank|**100** 🔴|Max — premium selling strongly favored|
|IV Regime|**HIGH**|Sell premium, not buy it|
|VIX|17.0 (44th %ile)|Calm macro — no systemic fear premium|

>

# 2. 📊 Technical Analysis — Bearish Signals Emerging[](http://localhost:8501/#step-1-iv-snapshot-confirmed)

# Daily (Primary):[](http://localhost:8501/#multi-timeframe-signal-summary)

* **SMA Stack:** Bullish (200 < 50 < 20) — BUT this is a *lagging* signal after a massive run
* **RSI:** 68.2 — approaching overbought territory (>70 = signal)
* **Stochastics:** 87.4K / 87.6D — **deeply overbought** ⚠️
* **MACD Histogram:** Turned **negative (-0.35)** — momentum rolling over
* **BB %B:** 0.82 — price riding the upper band, a mean-reversion zone

# 4H:[](http://localhost:8501/#trend-classification)

* **OBV:** Falling — distribution phase; buyers not confirming the rally
* **Stochastics:** Overbought (82/87)
* Volume: 14% of average — **thin, suspect rally**

# 1H (Fastest Signal):[](http://localhost:8501/#key-levels)

* **MACD:** Bearish crossover confirmed (-1.10 histogram)
* **Stochastics:** Turned bearish (41/44)
* **OBV:** Falling — institutional selling pressure intraday

# Key Levels:[](http://localhost:8501/#gex-analysis)

|Level|Type|Notes|
|:-|:-|:-|
|613.27/613.27/607.71|**Resistance**|R1/R2 pivot; 52-week high = $612.30|
|$600|**Round number resistance**|High OI call wall (2,920 OI)|
|$590|**Current spot / ATM**|ATM support|
|$583|**Support (S2)**|First meaningful support|
|$560|**Major support**|Round number + historical level|

>

# 3. 🗓️ Calendar Checks — Clear to Trade[](http://localhost:8501/#step-2-technical-analysis)

* **Earnings:** No confirmed earnings date in the next 60 days — outside no-trade window ✅
* **Economic Events (next 14 days):** No major CPI/FOMC releases flagged ✅
* **Existing SMH Positions:** **None** — account is flat, full $2,500 budget available ✅

>

# 4. 🎯 Recommended Strategy: Bear Call Spread[](http://localhost:8501/#step-3-portfolio-and-macro-context)

# Why Bear Call Spread (not Long Put or Bear Put Spread)?[](http://localhost:8501/#portfolio-check)

|Factor|Verdict|
|:-|:-|
|IVR = 100|**Sell premium**, don't buy it — long puts are expensive|
|Bearish bias|Need upside cap, not downside momentum play|
|Defined risk|$2,500 budget enforced — no naked calls|
|Theta working for us|50 DTE, theta positive from day 1|
|Macro: VIX normal|No tail-risk inflation — ideal for credit spreads|

# 📋 Trade Specification[](http://localhost:8501/#macro-context)

    STRATEGY:    Bear Call Spread (Credit)
    UNDERLYING:  SMH @ $590.14
    EXPIRATION:  July 17, 2026 (50 DTE)
    
    SHORT LEG:   Sell 4x SMH Jul-17 $620 Call
                 Bid/Ask: $30.55 / $31.80 → Target mid: $31.17
                 Delta: 0.44 → ~0.35 effective after spread
                 IV: 49.7%
    
    LONG LEG:    Buy 4x SMH Jul-17 $640 Call
                 Bid/Ask: $23.85 / $24.80 → Target mid: $24.32
                 Delta: 0.37
                 IV: 49.2%
    
    SPREAD WIDTH:  $20 per spread
    NET CREDIT:    ~$6.85 per spread ($31.17 - $24.32)
                   Target fill: $6.50–$7.00 (use limit at mid)
    CONTRACTS:     4

# 💰 P&L Profile (4 Contracts)[](http://localhost:8501/#sector-analysis)

|Metric|Value|
|:-|:-|
|**Max Profit**|6.85×100×4=∗∗6.85×100×4=∗∗2,740\*\*|
|**Max Loss**|(20−20−6.85) × 100 × 4 = **$5,260**|
|**Risk Within Budget**|Use 2 contracts → Max loss = **$2,630** ✅|
|**Breakeven**|620+620+6.85 = **$626.85**|
|**Distance to Short Strike**|620−620−590 = **$30 (5.1% OTM)**|
|**Distance to Breakeven**|626.85−626.85−590 = **$36.85 (6.2% OTM)**|
|**Probability of Profit**|\~**70–72%**|
|**Credit as % of Width**|34.3% — solid for a $20-wide spread|

>

# 📐 Net Greeks (2 contracts)[](http://localhost:8501/#strategy-selection-matrix)

|Greek|Value|Meaning|
|:-|:-|:-|
|**Delta**|\-0.14|Slightly bearish — profits from stagnation or decline|
|**Theta**|\+$0.25/day|You earn \~$17.50/week in time decay|
|**Vega**|\-0.08|IV drop benefits you (likely as rally fades)|
|**Gamma**|\-0.001|Low — spread won't blow up on small moves|

# 5. 📉 Backtest Reality Check (2 Years, 26 Trades)[](http://localhost:8501/#step-4-strategy-recommendation)

|Metric|Result|Assessment|
|:-|:-|:-|
|Win Rate|**61.5%**|✅ Above 60% threshold|
|Avg Win|\+$169|Decent per-contract|
|Avg Loss|\-$336|2:1 loss-to-win ratio|
|Expectancy|**-$25/trade**|⚠️ Negative — edge is thin|
|Profit Factor|0.80|⚠️ <1.0 — marginally unprofitable systemically|
|Max Drawdown|\-$2,294|Significant at 1-contract sizing|
|Sharpe Ratio|\-1.59|🔴 Poor risk-adjusted returns in backtests|

>

>

# 6. 📋 Entry/Exit Plan[](http://localhost:8501/#recommended-trades-two-leg-approach)

# Entry — Scale In (2 Tranches)[](http://localhost:8501/#why-not-long-call-pure-debit)

**Tranche 1 — TODAY (50% of position = 1–2 contracts)**

* Condition: Fill at $6.50 or better credit (limit order at mid)
* Strikes: Short 620/Long620/*Long*640
* Expiry: July 17, 2026
* Order type: Limit, GTC, at natural mid-price

**Tranche 2 — Add on Strength (remaining 1–2 contracts)**

* Trigger: SMH rallies to 595–595–600 (giving better distance OTM) OR IV expands 5%+ from today's level
* Alternative trigger: SMH closes below $583 (S2 support) — confirming bearish momentum
* Price target: Same strikes at $7.00+ credit (better risk/reward)

# Active Management Rules[](http://localhost:8501/#why-not-a-pure-premium-selling-structure)

|Scenario|Action|
|:-|:-|
|**Profit: Spread decays to $3.43 (50% profit)**|✅ **Close the position — take the win**|
|**Profit: Spread decays to $1.71 (75% profit)**|Close remaining, book near-full profit|
|**Loss: Spread expands to $13.70 (2× credit)**|🛑 **Stop loss — close immediately**|
|**SMH closes above $620 for 2 consecutive days**|🛑 Close regardless of spread price|
|**SMH closes above $612 (52-week high breakout)**|⚠️ Reduce position by 50%, reassess|
|**DTE reaches 15 (July 2)**|Close if not at profit target — avoid gamma risk|
|**IV collapses >15%**|Close early — vega will reprice spread cheaper, capture gain|

# Timeline[](http://localhost:8501/#primary-bull-call-spread-debit-leverages-ivr-0)

    Entry:         May 27–28, 2026  (50 DTE)
    Manage active: May 28 – July 2, 2026
    Target close:  ~July 1–2, 2026  (15 DTE)
    Hard expiry:   July 17, 2026    (do NOT hold to expiry)
    Expected hold: 30–35 days

# 7. 🚦 Risk Summary & Final Verdict[](http://localhost:8501/#7-risk-summary-and-final-verdict)

**What needs to go right:**

* SMH stays below $620 (only needs to be flat or down 5%)
* No surprise semiconductor catalyst (NVDA blowout earnings, AI capex surge, geopolitical chip deal)
* Time decay works normally over the 35-day hold

**What can go wrong:**

* SMH follows XLK's +14.2% monthly momentum higher — a breakout above $612 is the key danger
* Earnings of major holdings (NVDA, TSMC, AMD) trigger a gap above short strike
* Macro surprise (Fed dovish pivot, trade war resolution) reignites risk rally

**Position Confidence: 6.5/10** — Strong IV setup, clean technical picture, but fighting an underlying that's in a strong uptrend. Keep sizing conservative (1–2 contracts), honor stops religiously, and book 50% profit without hesitation.

>

comments.author: Fun-Society-1763
comments.body: This is exactly the kind of use case where AI shines for discretionary traders. You don't necessarily need it to take the trades, you just need it to digest the overnight data, news, and key levels so you can sit down at the open with a clear bias. If you don't want to code this whole data-ingestion pipeline from scratch, QuantPlace is a great environment to build this kind of AI analyst. You can hook it up to run your specific analysis routine every morning before the bell so you just get a clean summary output.
